report_name: Report Details "Operation HookedWing," a Four-Year Phishing Campaign Targeting 500+ Organizations

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Victims of Operation HookedWing received spear-phishing emails containing a link that redirected them to a credential harvesting page.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: The campaign used high-fidelity clones of Microsoft Outlook login portals, often personalized with the victim's company logo or email address, to enhance legitimacy and harvest credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Web Services
attack_patterns.description: The campaign used legitimate web services like GitHub for hosting phishing kits and landing pages to bypass security filters.

attack_patterns.name: Server
attack_patterns.description: The campaign used compromised servers to host phishing pages as part of its infrastructure.

campaigns.name: Operation HookedWing
campaigns.description: A persistent phishing campaign active since at least 2022, targeting over 500 organizations in critical sectors to steal Microsoft Outlook credentials using lures in English and French.
campaigns.objective: Steal over 2,000 user credentials from organizations in critical infrastructure, aviation, government, and financial sectors.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure Sector Organizations
identities.description: Organizations within the critical infrastructure and energy sectors targeted by the Operation HookedWing phishing campaign.

identities.name: Aviation Sector Organizations
identities.description: Organizations within the aviation sector targeted by the Operation HookedWing phishing campaign.

identities.name: Government Sector Organizations
identities.description: Government organizations targeted by the Operation HookedWing phishing campaign.

identities.name: Financial Sector Organizations
identities.description: Organizations within the financial sector targeted by the Operation HookedWing phishing campaign.

infrastructure.name: Operation HookedWing Phishing Hosting Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A network of over 100 GitHub domains and compromised servers used to host credential harvesting landing pages.

infrastructure.name: Operation HookedWing C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Two dozen command-and-control servers used to receive credentials exfiltrated from victims of the phishing campaign.

indicators.name: Potential Phishing Host on GitHub.io
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Phishing Host on GitHub.io
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to domains hosted on github.io, which was used in Operation HookedWing to host phishing pages.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/over-500-organizations-hit-in-years-long-phishing-campaign/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1583.006
  - attack.resource_development
logsource:
  category: proxy
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of GitHub Pages for documentation, blogs, or project websites.
level: low
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith: '.github.io'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to domains hosted on github.io. While legitimate use is common, the Operation HookedWing campaign leveraged this service to host phishing pages, making traffic to these domains potentially suspicious.
